session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8887 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it to classify the PR feedback for PR #8887.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null

tool_use.param: Show file restructuring in current branch vs main

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8887, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8887", "pr_title": "Wire incremental dispatch into TUI (Objective #8470, Phase 2)", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yuHTO", "path": "src/erk/tui/operations/workers.py", "line": 430, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Tripwires Review**: Using tempfile for AI workflow file - should use .erk/scratch storage

This code uses `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile` to create a temp file in `/tmp/`, but according to **docs/learned/planning/scratch-storage.md**, AI workflow files should use `.erk/scratch/sessions/<session-id>/` storage instead.

The plan markdown file is an AI workflow intermediate file. Use the scratch storage API:

```python
from erk_shared.scratch.scratch import write_scratch_file

file_path = write_scratch_file(
 content=plan_content,
 session_id=session_id, # Need to pass session_id parameter
 suffix=".md",
 prefix="incremental-plan-",
)
```

See: docs/learned/planning/scratch-storage.md tripwire "writing to /tmp/" and "creating temp files for AI workflows"", "created_at": "2026-03-07T02:06:21Z" } ] } ], "discussion_comments": [ { "id": 4015212136, "author": "schrockn", "body": "Plan Queued for Implementation

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-06 18:05:40 PT

Found 0 violations. All code is compliant with dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL over EAFP - No violations found
✅ Imports (absolute, module-level) - All compliant
✅ Path operations (exists before resolve/unlink) - Correct usage at workers.py:476
✅ Exception handling - Proper boundaries and exception chains
✅ No default parameter values - All new signatures compliant
✅ Keyword-only arguments - Proper for multi-parameter functions
✅ Frozen dataclasses - No new dataclass definitions
✅ Properties must be O(1) - No violations
✅ No re-exports (__all__) - No violations
✅ Subprocess operations - No subprocess calls added

### Violations Summary
None detected.

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/tui/actions/palette.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/tui/operations/workers.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/tui/screens/plan_input_screen.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/commands/launch/test_launch_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/tui/app/test_incremental_dispatch.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/tui/screens/test_plan_input_screen.py`: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-06 18:05:40 PT**: All code compliant with dignified-python standards. Imports proper (absolute, module-level). Path handling correct with exists() checks. LBYL patterns throughout.

</details>", "body": "<!-- dignified-code-simplifier-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-06 18:05:48 PT

Found 0 violations across 10 files. All code follows dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL vs EAFP - Properly using defensive checks with getattr() for optional attributes
✅ Path operations - Correct .exists() checks before .unlink()
✅ Import organization - All absolute imports, properly organized
✅ Properties O(1) - No I/O or iteration in properties
✅ Default parameters - No default parameter values used
✅ Pathlib usage - Consistent use of pathlib.Path
✅ Exception handling - Proper error boundaries and LBYL patterns

### Violations Summary
None detected.

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py`: 0 violations (deletion only)
- `src/erk/tui/actions/palette.py`: 0 violations (new import and handler)
- `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py`: 0 violations (new function and command)
- `src/erk/tui/operations/workers.py`: 0 violations (new async method with proper Path handling)
- `src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py`: 0 violations (new conditional)
- `src/erk/tui/screens/plan_input_screen.py`: 0 violations (new modal screen)
- `tests/commands/launch/test_launch_cmd.py`: 0 violations (assertion deletion)
- `tests/tui/app/test_incremental_dispatch.py`: 0 violations (new test file)
- `tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py`: 0 violations (new test cases)
- `tests/tui/screens/test_plan_input_screen.py`: 0 violations (new test file)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-06 18:05:48 PT**: Initial review - no violations detected across 10 files

</details>", "body": "**Test Coverage Review** on src/erk/tui/operations/workers.py line 422:

New async method `_incremental_dispatch_async` (64 lines) lacks Layer 4 business logic tests. Erk requires Layer 4 tests for all feature code. This method implements the core incremental dispatch flow including tempfile I/O, subprocess invocation, error handling, and thread-safe notifications.",
